primary_modes.rationale: The task is fundamentally a coding-standards / naming-convention enforcement exercise in a Dart/Flutter library: renaming boolean props from is/has-prefixed forms to descriptive adjectives, adding naming-convention tests, and adding coverage-tracking JSON artefacts. All of these are developer-ergonomics concerns that live squarely in DX.

secondary_modes.rationale: New test files and REQ coverage-tracking JSON files (REQ-0015, REQ-0016, REQ-0017) must be created from scratch, which constitutes net-new deliverable artefacts beyond a pure refactor.
Several renamed props (moreAvailable, olderMessagesAvailable, keyboardVisible) belong to public widget APIs consumed by app developers; a clear, readable API surface is a UX-of-the-library concern.

generic.rationale: Clearly document the naming-convention rule (no is/has prefix; use descriptive adjective/noun) and enumerate all 8 specific violations to provide unambiguous scope.
Already partially supplied; must be formalised to include: all renames applied, naming-convention test file present and passing, and REQ-0015/0016/0017 tracking JSONs committed.
Each of the 8 file:line violations needs an explicit pass/fail criterion, plus criteria for the new test file and the three missing coverage JSON files.
No naming-convention tests currently exist; test cases must be designed to assert that all public boolean parameters in the library match the adjective/noun convention, preventing regressions.
Renaming public API props is a breaking change for any downstream consumer; risk of silent breakage in consumers not yet on null-safety or with dynamic reflection must be captured.
A deprecation-alias strategy (keeping old names with @deprecated) may be needed if consumers cannot migrate immediately; rollback plan should be documented.
Surface assumptions such as 'no external package consumers will break' or 'a CHANGELOG / migration guide is out of scope' so the team can validate them.
Capture any version-bump policy (semver major vs minor), lint-rule configuration constraints, and CI gate requirements for coverage JSON.
Clarify that this task does not address other naming conventions (e.g. method naming, event handlers) to prevent scope creep.
Useful to state the measurable goal: bring boolean-naming compliance from 62% to 100% as tracked by the requirement atoms.

specialized.rationale: Core deliverable: document and enforce the boolean naming convention (descriptive adjective/noun, no is/has prefix) as a codified ergonomics rule for the library's public API.
A lint script or custom analysis rule that auto-detects is/has-prefixed booleans could be valuable tooling, but is optional for this fix task.
The renames affect a public Flutter widget/data-class SDK surface; the naming convention decision should be recorded as an SDK design guideline to govern future additions.

recommended_specialist_agents.rationale: Owns the rename refactor across all 8 violations, writes the naming-convention test file, and defines/enforces the lint or analysis rule going forward.
Designs the naming-convention test cases, validates that the new tests cover REQ-0015/0016/0017, and confirms implementation reaches 100% on the requirement atom.
Produces the migration guide / CHANGELOG entry for downstream consumers affected by the breaking rename of public boolean props, and documents the convention in contributing guidelines.

Reviews the public API surface changes for OiInfiniteScroll, OiListView, OiActivityFeed, OiChat, OiFileNode, OiPlatformData, and OiGradient to ensure the renames are consistent with overall widget API design.
Only relevant if any of the renamed props (e.g. OiPlatformData.keyboardVisible) are serialised/deserialised over a wire protocol and a corresponding backend contract must be updated.

assumptions: The library follows semantic versioning; renaming public props constitutes at minimum a minor (or major, if no deprecation aliases) version bump.
No external published package consumers exist that cannot be updated in the same PR/sprint, OR a @deprecated alias strategy is acceptable to smooth migration.
The existing test suite runs in CI and a new naming-convention test file placed in the standard test/ directory will be automatically picked up.
Coverage tracking JSON files follow the schema already established for REQ-0012, REQ-0013, REQ-0014 and can be created by copying that structure.
Internal state fields (e.g. _OiActivityFeedState.hasMore) that are not part of the public API should still be renamed for internal consistency, but do not constitute a breaking change.
The 62% current implementation figure is derived from the REQ-0015/0016/0017 coverage tracking and will reach 100% once all 8 violations and missing artefacts are resolved.
No code generation (e.g. freezed, json_serializable) is producing the is/has-prefixed booleans; the renaming is purely manual.

open_questions.question: Should deprecated aliases (e.g. `bool get hasMore => moreAvailable`) be retained for one release cycle to avoid immediate breakage for consumers?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Without aliases, any downstream app using `hasMore`, `hasOlderMessages`, `isFolder`, `isKeyboardVisible`, or `isLinear` will fail to compile after the upgrade, making this a hard breaking change.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Add @deprecated aliases for one minor version, then remove in the next release.

open_questions.question: What is the exact schema and required fields for the REQ-0015, REQ-0016, and REQ-0017 coverage tracking JSON files?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Creating malformed or incomplete JSON files will not satisfy the EPIC-0003 tracking gate and may cause CI to fail.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Mirror the structure of the existing REQ-0012, REQ-0013, REQ-0014 files already committed in EPIC-0003.

open_questions.question: Should the naming-convention test be a static analysis / AST-based test or a runtime reflection test?
open_questions.why_it_matters: A static/AST approach catches violations at build time and is more robust; a runtime approach is easier to write but less reliable for a naming convention.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Use a static analysis custom lint rule (e.g. via custom_lint or dart analyze) so violations are caught in the IDE and CI without running the app.

open_questions.question: Does OiFileNode.isFolder affect any JSON serialisation layer (e.g. a REST API response mapped to this class)?
open_questions.why_it_matters: If the JSON key 'isFolder' is consumed from a backend, renaming the Dart field to 'folder' without updating the JSON key or the serialisation annotation will cause silent data-mapping bugs.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Assume no backend coupling; the field is UI-only. Confirm with the backend team before shipping.

open_questions.question: Is there a timeline or version milestone by which this fix must land (e.g. tied to a public release or design-system audit)?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Determines whether a fast single-PR approach (rename + break) or a two-phase deprecation approach is appropriate.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Target the next scheduled minor release; use deprecation aliases in the interim.
